ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bun class; no specific sesame-filled steamed bun entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX covers steamed bread and buns as a category; no sesame-filled variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for steamed sesame buns. None assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One steamed sesame bun weighing approximately 55 g, equivalent to one piece from a standard 6-piece frozen pack retailing at SGD 14.50.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Bun with Sesame Filling — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite estimate for filled steamed wheat bun
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for black-sesame-filled steamed bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy entries for plain steamed wheat bun (approx. 70% by weight) and black sesame paste filling (approx. 30% by weight), cross-referenced with USDA FDC entry 'Bread, steamed, Chinese style' (FDC ID 168012) and black sesame paste nutritional profile. Energy and macros are consistent with published values for similar dim sum steamed buns in the literature.

gi_source.title: Steamed wheat bun (Chinese-style) — analogous GI estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for steamed white bread / Chinese steamed bun
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values of 68 (±7) for Chinese steamed white bread (mantou) tested against glucose reference. A sesame-filled steamed bun contains a significant proportion of black sesame paste (fat and fibre-rich), which moderately lowers the overall GI relative to plain mantou. An estimated GI of 65 is applied, consistent with medium-GI classification. GL per serving calculated as (65 × 26.1 g available carbohydrate per 55 g bun) / 100 ≈ 17.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100 g; ~115 mg per bun)
hypertension.guidance: Each steamed sesame bun contributes approximately 115 mg sodium; individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to one bun and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as soy sauce dips to keep total meal sodium within the recommended 2000 mg/day ceiling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~65) and moderate carbohydrate load (~26 g per bun)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 65 and a glycemic load of ~17 per bun, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict intake to one bun per sitting, pair with a protein or non-starchy vegetable side dish to blunt postprandial glucose response, and monitor blood glucose accordingly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content with favourable unsaturated-to-saturated ratio; black sesame provides beneficial polyunsaturated fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The black sesame filling contributes polyunsaturated fatty acids (sesamin, sesamolin) and plant sterols that are broadly cardioprotective; saturated fat per bun is low (~1 g), making occasional consumption compatible with a heart-healthy dietary pattern when overall saturated fat and sodium intake are controlled.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Sesame Buns (芝麻包, Zhī ma bāo) are soft, pillowy Chinese steamed buns filled with a rich, fragrant black sesame paste. The bun dough is made from refined wheat flour leavened with yeast, giving it a light, fluffy texture with a slightly chewy bite. The filling is a smooth, dense paste of ground black sesame seeds sweetened with sugar, delivering a nutty, toasty, and mildly sweet flavour profile. This product is sold frozen in a 6-piece pack and is associated with Din Tai Fung, a Michelin-recognised Taiwanese restaurant chain renowned for its dim sum and Taiwanese-Chinese cuisine. The buns are steamed from frozen or after thawing, making them a convenient home preparation of a classic dim sum item. Nutritionally, each bun (approximately 55 g) provides around 162 kcal, 26 g of carbohydrate, 4.7 g of fat, and 3.7 g of protein. The black sesame filling contributes dietary fibre, polyunsaturated fatty acids, calcium, iron, and magnesium. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is moderate at approximately 115 mg per bun. Clinically, these buns are suitable for occasional consumption within a balanced diet but require portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or those monitoring caloric intake. They are vegetarian-friendly but contain gluten and sesame allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for black-sesame-filled steamed bun was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for plain steamed wheat bun and black sesame paste in approximate proportions consistent with the product description. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for Chinese steamed white bread (mantou), adjusted downward for sesame filling. Confidence is medium due to the estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated composites; no exact product-level nutritional panel was available from an approved source. The Din Tai Fung product label was not accessible as an approved source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero for a yeast-leavened steamed bun without hydrogenated fats; this is consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for similar products. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as the product is plant-based (no eggs or dairy in standard formulation); naturally zero for this product type. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a grain-based baked/steamed product with no fruit or vegetable components. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the composite source estimate for this product type. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary composite source. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported; product is plant-based and B12 is not expected. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source, though black sesame does contain tocopherols; a future revision with a direct product panel is recommended. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source. 12. Halal status is marked false as Din Tai Fung Singapore is not certified halal; this should be verified against current restaurant certification. 13. Serving size of 55 g is estimated as total pack weight divided by 6 pieces; actual piece weight may vary. 14. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; a direct GI test of this specific product has not been published in the University of Sydney GI Database.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as required for Chinese cuisine served in Singapore context.
Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with cross-consistency verified.
Estimated nutrient values using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite approach with full documentation.
Applied analogous GI estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (mantou/Chinese steamed white bread) with downward adjustment for sesame filling.
Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes.
Documented all 0.0 values (trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg) with zero-justification phrases in notes.
Selected FoodOn closest_parent and FoodEx2 closest_parent with gap notes; SNOMED CT set to none with gap note.
